title: Acianthera obscura
taxonomy: Acianthera obscura is classified as a species of orchid in the family Orchidaceae. It belongs to the subtribe Pleurothallidinae and is native to South America. The genus Acianthera is a group of orchids that are characterized by their small size and unique flower structures. The classification of Acianthera obscura is based on its distinctive characteristics, including its small, white flowers and its growth habit. This orchid species is often found growing in the understory of tropical forests.
uses: Acianthera obscura has been traditionally used in folk medicine, and some studies suggest its potential in treating various health issues.
